lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪਿ ਕਰੇ ਕਿਸੁ ਆਖੀਐ ਵੇਖੈ ਵਡਿਆਈ ॥੭॥
lines.translit: aap kare kis aakheeai vekhai vaddiaaee |7|
lines.english: He Himself acts; who else should be mentioned? He beholds His own greatness. ||7||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇਹੀ ਸੇਵ ਕਰਾਈਐ ਕਰਣੀ ਭੀ ਸਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: jehee sev karaaeeai karanee bhee saaee |
lines.english: Whatever service the Lord causes us to do, that is just what we do.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਕੀ ਸੇਵਾ ਸੋ ਕਰੇ ਜਿਸੁ ਆਪਿ ਕਰਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: gur kee sevaa so kare jis aap karaae |
lines.english: He alone serves the Guru, whom the Lord Himself inspires to do so.

lines.gurmukhi: ਰੂੜੋ ਠਾਕੁਰ ਮਾਹਰੋ ਰੂੜੀ ਗੁਰਬਾਣੀ ॥
lines.translit: roorro tthaakur maaharo roorree gurabaanee |
lines.english: Beautiful is the Supreme Lord and Master, and beautiful is the Word of the Guru's Bani.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਸਿਰੁ ਦੇ ਛੂਟੀਐ ਦਰਗਹ ਪਤਿ ਪਾਏ ॥੮॥੧੮॥
lines.translit: naanak sir de chhootteeai daragah pat paae |8|18|
lines.english: O Nanak, offering his head, one is emancipated, and honored in the Court of the Lord. ||8||18||

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਸਾ ਮਹਲਾ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: aasaa mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: Aasaa, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਡੈ ਭਾਗਿ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਮਿਲੈ ਪਾਈਐ ਪਦੁ ਨਿਰਬਾਣੀ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: vaddai bhaag satigur milai paaeeai pad nirabaanee |1|
lines.english: By great good fortune, one meets the True Guru, and the supreme status of Nirvaanaa is obtained. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੈ ਓਲੑਗੀਆ ਓਲੑਗੀ ਹਮ ਛੋਰੂ ਥਾਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: mai olageea olagee ham chhoroo thaare |
lines.english: I am the lowest slave of Your slaves; I am Your most humble servant.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਉ ਤੂੰ ਰਾਖਹਿ ਤਿਉ ਰਹਾ ਮੁਖਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਹਮਾਰੇ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: jiau toon raakheh tiau rahaa mukh naam hamaare |1| rahaau |
lines.english: As You keep me, I live. Your Name is in my mouth. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਦਰਸਨ ਕੀ ਪਿਆਸਾ ਘਣੀ ਭਾਣੈ ਮਨਿ ਭਾਈਐ ॥
lines.translit: darasan kee piaasaa ghanee bhaanai man bhaaeeai |
lines.english: I have such a great thirst for the Blessed Vision of Your Darshan; my mind accepts Your Will, and so You are pleased with me.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੇਰੇ ਠਾਕੁਰ ਹਾਥਿ ਵਡਿਆਈਆ ਭਾਣੈ ਪਤਿ ਪਾਈਐ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: mere tthaakur haath vaddiaaeea bhaanai pat paaeeai |2|
lines.english: Greatness is in the Hands of my Lord and Master; by His Will, honor is obtained. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਚਉ ਦੂਰਿ ਨ ਜਾਣੀਐ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਹੈ ਸੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: saachau door na jaaneeai antar hai soee |
lines.english: Do not think that the True Lord is far away; He is deep within.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਹੋਇ ਨਿਹਾਲੀਐ ਇਉ ਕੀਮਤਿ ਪਾਈ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: guramukh hoe nihaaleeai iau keemat paaee |4|
lines.english: Becoming Gurmukh, one beholds Him, and so, His value is appraised. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਹ ਦੇਖਾ ਤਹ ਰਵਿ ਰਹੇ ਕਿਨਿ ਕੀਮਤਿ ਹੋਈ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: jeh dekhaa teh rav rahe kin keemat hoee |3|
lines.english: Wherever I look, there I find Him pervading; how can I estimate His value? ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪਿ ਕਰੇ ਆਪੇ ਹਰੇ ਵੇਖੈ ਵਡਿਆਈ ॥
lines.translit: aap kare aape hare vekhai vaddiaaee |
lines.english: He Himself does, and He Himself undoes. He Himself beholds His glorious greatness.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੀਵਦਿਆ ਲਾਹਾ ਮਿਲੈ ਗੁਰ ਕਾਰ ਕਮਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: jeevadiaa laahaa milai gur kaar kamaavai |
lines.english: So earn your profits while you are alive, by serving the Guru.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੂਰਬਿ ਹੋਵੈ ਲਿਖਿਆ ਤਾ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਪਾਵੈ ॥੫॥
lines.translit: poorab hovai likhiaa taa satigur paavai |5|
lines.english: If it is so pre-ordained, then one finds the True Guru. ||5||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨਮੁਖ ਤੋਟਾ ਨਿਤ ਹੈ ਭਰਮਹਿ ਭਰਮਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: manamukh tottaa nit hai bharameh bharamaae |
lines.english: The self-willed manmukhs continually lose, and wander around, deluded by doubt.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨਮੁਖੁ ਅੰਧੁ ਨ ਚੇਤਈ ਕਿਉ ਦਰਸਨੁ ਪਾਏ ॥੬॥
lines.translit: manamukh andh na chetee kiau darasan paae |6|
lines.english: The blind manmukhs do not remember the Lord; how can they obtain the Blessed Vision of His Darshan? ||6||

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਭੇਟੇ ਪਾਰਸੁ ਭਏ ਜੋਤੀ ਜੋਤਿ ਮਿਲਾਏ ॥੭॥
lines.translit: gur bhette paaras bhe jotee jot milaae |7|
lines.english: Meeting the Guru, one becomes invaluable; his light merges into the Light. ||7||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਹਿਨਿਸਿ ਰਹੈ ਨਿਰਾਲਮੋ ਕਾਰ ਧੁਰ ਕੀ ਕਰਣੀ ॥
lines.translit: ahinis rahai niraalamo kaar dhur kee karanee |
lines.english: Day and night, he remains detached, and serves the Primal Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਾ ਜਗਿ ਆਇਆ ਜਾਣੀਐ ਸਾਚੈ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: taa jag aaeaa jaaneeai saachai liv laae |
lines.english: One's coming into the world is judged worthwhile only if one lovingly attunes oneself to the True Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਨਾਮਿ ਸੰਤੋਖੀਆ ਰਾਤੇ ਹਰਿ ਚਰਣੀ ॥੮॥੧੯॥
lines.translit: naanak naam santokheea raate har charanee |8|19|
lines.english: O Nanak, those who are imbued with the Lord's Lotus Feet, are content with the Naam, the Name of the Lord. ||8||19||

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਸਾ ਮਹਲਾ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: aasaa mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: Aasaa, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਕੀ ਅਰਦਾਸਿ ਹੈ ਸਚ ਨਾਮਿ ਸੁਹੇਲਾ ॥
lines.translit: naanak kee aradaas hai sach naam suhelaa |
lines.english: This is Nanak's prayer, that he may be adorned with the True Name.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੇਤਾ ਆਖਣੁ ਆਖੀਐ ਤਾ ਕੇ ਅੰਤ ਨ ਜਾਣਾ ॥
lines.translit: ketaa aakhan aakheeai taa ke ant na jaanaa |
lines.english: No matter how much one may describe the Lord, His limits still cannot be known.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੈ ਨਿਧਰਿਆ ਧਰ ਏਕ ਤੂੰ ਮੈ ਤਾਣੁ ਸਤਾਣਾ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: mai nidhariaa dhar ek toon mai taan sataanaa |1|
lines.english: I am without any support; You, O Lord, are my only Support; You are my almighty power. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੁ ਗਇਆ ਸੋਝੀ ਪਈ ਗੁਰਸਬਦੀ ਮੇਲਾ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: aap geaa sojhee pee gurasabadee melaa |1| rahaau |
lines.english: When self-conceit is eradicated, and understanding is obtained, one meets the Lord, through the Word of the Guru's Shabad. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਉਮੈ ਗਰਬੁ ਗਵਾਈਐ ਪਾਈਐ ਵੀਚਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: haumai garab gavaaeeai paaeeai veechaar |
lines.english: Abandoning egotism and pride, one obtains contemplative understanding.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਹਿਨਿਸਿ ਨਾਮਿ ਸੰਤੋਖੀਆ ਸੇਵਾ ਸਚੁ ਸਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: ahinis naam santokheea sevaa sach saaee |
lines.english: Day and night, remain content with the Naam, the Name of the Lord; that is the true service.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਹਿਬ ਸਿਉ ਮਨੁ ਮਾਨਿਆ ਦੇ ਸਾਚੁ ਅਧਾਰੁ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: saahib siau man maaniaa de saach adhaar |2|
lines.english: When the mind surrenders to the Lord Master, He bestows the support of the Truth. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹੁਕਮਿ ਰਜਾਈ ਜੋ ਚਲੈ ਸੋ ਪਵੈ ਖਜਾਨੈ ॥
lines.translit: hukam rajaaee jo chalai so pavai khajaanai |
lines.english: One who follows the Command of the Lord's Will is taken into the Lord's Treasury.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਾ ਕਉ ਬਿਘਨੁ ਨ ਲਾਗਈ ਚਾਲੈ ਹੁਕਮਿ ਰਜਾਈ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: taa kau bighan na laagee chaalai hukam rajaaee |3|
lines.english: No misfortune troubles one who follows the Command of the Lord's Will. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਖੋਟੇ ਠਵਰ ਨ ਪਾਇਨੀ ਰਲੇ ਜੂਠਾਨੈ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: khotte tthavar na paaeinee rale jootthaanai |4|
lines.english: The counterfeit find no place there; they are mixed with the false ones. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਿਤ ਨਿਤ ਖਰਾ ਸਮਾਲੀਐ ਸਚੁ ਸਉਦਾ ਪਾਈਐ ॥
lines.translit: nit nit kharaa samaaleeai sach saudaa paaeeai |
lines.english: Forever and ever, the genuine coins are treasured; with them, the true merchandise is purchased.

lines.gurmukhi: ਖੋਟੇ ਨਦਰਿ ਨ ਆਵਨੀ ਲੇ ਅਗਨਿ ਜਲਾਈਐ ॥੫॥
lines.translit: khotte nadar na aavanee le agan jalaaeeai |5|
lines.english: The false ones are not seen in the Lord's Treasury; they are seized and cast into the fire again. ||5||

lines.gurmukhi: ਏਕੋ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਬਿਰਖੁ ਹੈ ਫਲੁ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤੁ ਹੋਈ ॥੬॥
lines.translit: eko amrit birakh hai fal amrit hoee |6|
lines.english: The One Lord is the tree of ambrosial nectar, which bears the ambrosial fruit. ||6||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨੀ ਆਤਮੁ ਚੀਨਿਆ ਪਰਮਾਤਮੁ ਸੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: jinee aatam cheeniaa paramaatam soee |
lines.english: Those who understand their own souls, are themselves the Supreme Soul.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਫਲੁ ਜਿਨੀ ਚਾਖਿਆ ਸਚਿ ਰਹੇ ਅਘਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: amrit fal jinee chaakhiaa sach rahe aghaaee |
lines.english: Those who taste the ambrosial fruit remain satisfied with Truth.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿੰਨਾ ਭਰਮੁ ਨ ਭੇਦੁ ਹੈ ਹਰਿ ਰਸਨ ਰਸਾਈ ॥੭॥
lines.translit: tinaa bharam na bhed hai har rasan rasaaee |7|
lines.english: They have no doubt or sense of separation - their tongues taste the divine taste. ||7||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਉਗਣਿਆਰੇ ਕਉ ਗੁਣੁ ਨਾਨਕੈ ਸਚੁ ਮਿਲੈ ਵਡਾਈ ॥੮॥੨੦॥
lines.translit: aauganiaare kau gun naanakai sach milai vaddaaee |8|20|
lines.english: Please, grant virtue to Nanak, the virtueless one; bless him with the glorious greatness of the Truth. ||8||20||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹੁਕਮਿ ਸੰਜੋਗੀ ਆਇਆ ਚਲੁ ਸਦਾ ਰਜਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: hukam sanjogee aaeaa chal sadaa rajaaee |
lines.english: By His Command, and through your past actions, you came into the world; walk forever according to His Will.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਸਾ ਮਹਲਾ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: aasaa mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: Aasaa, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਲੋਕਾ ਦਾ ਕਿਆ ਜਾਇ ਜਾ ਤੁਧੁ ਭਾਣਿਆ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: lokaa daa kiaa jaae jaa tudh bhaaniaa |1|
lines.english: What would the people lose, if I became pleasing to You, O Lord? ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨੁ ਰਾਤਉ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਇ ਸਚੁ ਵਖਾਣਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: man raatau har naae sach vakhaaniaa |
lines.english: One whose mind is attuned to the Lord's Name speaks the truth.
